message:
chore(beads): Sync issue tracking state

Update local issue tracker JSONL with latest bead status changes, priority adjustments,
and issue resolutions from concurrent agent work.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-15yvf","title":"Task: Implement context pack caching layer","description":"# Task: Context Pack Caching

Cache generated context packs for reuse across ensemble runs.

## Implementation
```go
// internal/ensemble/context_cache.go
type ContextCache struct {
    CacheDir string
    TTL      time.Duration
}

type CachedPack struct {
    Pack        *ContextPack
    Hash        string
    CachedAt    time.Time
    ExpiresAt   time.Time
    HitCount    int
}

func NewContextCache(cacheDir string, ttl time.Duration) *ContextCache
func (c *ContextCache) Get(projectRoot string) (*ContextPack, bool)
func (c *ContextCache) Put(projectRoot string, pack *ContextPack) error
func (c *ContextCache) Invalidate(projectRoot string) error
func (c *ContextCache) InvalidateAll() error
func (c *ContextCache) ComputeHash(pack *ContextPack) string
func (c *ContextCache) IsStale(pack *CachedPack) bool
```

## Cache Key
```
key = sha256(project_root + git_head_commit + problem_statement)
```

## Invalidation Triggers
- Git HEAD changes (new commits)
- Problem statement changes
- User explicit invalidation (--no-cache)
- TTL expiration (default 1 hour)

## Cache Location
```
~/.cache/ntm/context-packs/
├── <hash1>.json
├── <hash2>.json
└── metadata.json
```

## Logging
- Log cache hits/misses
- Log invalidation reasons
- Track hit rate in metrics

## File: internal/ensemble/context_cache.go","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T08:44:52.122338321Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T17:56:14.500989735Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T17:56:14.500942756Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of bd-v5fqf which is already closed with implementation","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-15yvf","depends_on_id":"bd-2ouwm","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-23T08:44:52.150388368Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-15yvf","depends_on_id":"bd-dkxa0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T09:12:02.219284257Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-16291","title":"Add ntm controller command to launch a dedicated controller agent in pane 1 with preset prompt","description":"Add ntm controller command to launch a dedicated controller agent in pane 1 with preset prompt.

## Problem Being Solved

Multi-agent swarms benefit from having a "controller" agent that coordinates the others. Currently this requires manual setup.

## Command Design

```bash
ntm controller SESSION [--prompt FILE] [--agent-type cc|cod|gmi]
```

### Behavior
1. Attaches to SESSION
2. Finds or creates pane 1
3. Launches agent with controller-specific prompt
4. Default prompt includes session overview and coordination instructions

### Default Controller Prompt
```
You are the controller agent for session {SESSION}.
Other agents in this session: {AGENT_LIST}
Your role: coordinate work, prevent conflicts, ensure quality.
Use ntm robot commands to monitor other agents.
```

### Custom Prompt
User can provide custom prompt file with variables:
- {SESSION}: session name
- {AGENT_LIST}: list of other agents
- {PROJECT}: project directory
- {BEADS}: open beads (if bv available)

## Robot Mode API
ntm --robot-controller-spawn=SESSION [--prompt=FILE]
Returns: JSON with controller pane info

## Acceptance Criteria

1. ntm controller command exists and shows in help
2. Command spawns agent in pane 1 of specified session
3. Default prompt includes session name and agent list
4. --prompt flag accepts path to custom prompt file
5. --agent-type flag allows choosing agent type (default: cc)
6. Robot mode variant returns JSON with pane ID

## Unit Tests (80% coverage target)
- internal/cmd/controller_test.go
- Test prompt variable substitution
- Test pane selection logic

## E2E Test Script
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# [E2E-CONTROLLER] Controller Command Tests
LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-CONTROLLER]"
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Testing ntm controller command"
TEST_SESSION="e2e_ctrl_$$"
ntm spawn "$TEST_SESSION" --cc=2 --no-prompt
sleep 5
# Test controller spawn
ntm controller "$TEST_SESSION" --no-prompt 2>&1 || echo "WARN: controller command not yet implemented"
ntm kill "$TEST_SESSION" --force
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Controller test complete"
```","acceptance_criteria":"1. ntm controller command launches dedicated agent in pane 1
2. Preset prompt includes project context
3. Agent has elevated permissions for coordination
4. Integration with agent mail for swarm control
5. Unit tests for command parsing
6. E2E test for controller lifecycle","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-20T05:27:38.891522309Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T18:14:30.596523260Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-168bh","title":"Unit Tests: Watch mode loop and exit conditions","description":"## Test Coverage
Unit tests for watch mode loop and exit conditions.

## Test Cases

### Watch Loop
- Initial assignment pass executes
- Completion detected triggers reassignment
- Unblocked beads detected and assigned
- Poll interval respected

### Exit Conditions
- Ctrl+C (SIGINT) triggers graceful shutdown
- --stop-when-done exits when no beads
- All agents crashed exits with error
- No more idle agents waits

### Streaming Output
- Timestamps accurate
- Assignment events logged
- Completion events logged
- Unblock events logged

### Error Handling
- BV unavailable mid-loop
- Agent Mail unavailable
- Assignment failure recovery

### Edge Cases
- Zero beads to start
- All beads blocked
- Single agent scenario
- Very fast completions

## Logging
- Log each loop iteration
- Log event processing
- Log exit reason

## Test File

 {"id":"bd-16dhv","title":"Use /usr/bin/tmux full path for reliable Enter key sending","description":"## Problem

When running tmux send-keys commands from certain shell environments (particularly when zsh tmux plugin is active), the bare `tmux` command gets intercepted by shell functions/aliases, causing:
- Commands fail silently or produce errors like `_zsh_tmux_plugin_run: command not found`
- Enter key not actually sent to panes
- Users see success messages but agents don't receive input

## Root Cause

The zsh tmux plugin defines functions that wrap tmux, and when running from within tmux (or from scripts sourced in zsh), these can interfere with direct tmux calls.

## Solution

Use the full path `/usr/bin/tmux` (or `$(which tmux)` resolved at startup) for ALL tmux operations throughout ntm, especially:
- `send-keys` commands
- `capture-pane` commands  
- Any command that needs to reliably communicate with tmux

## Files to Update

- All Go files that shell out to tmux
- Ensure tmux binary path is resolved once at startup and used consistently

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All tmux calls use full path (not bare `tmux`)
- [ ] Works correctly in zsh with tmux plugin active
- [ ] Works from within tmux sessions
- [ ] Enter key reliably sent to all panes
- [ ] Add test to verify tmux path resolution","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"claude-opus","created_at":"2026-01-22T01:05:24.907919887Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T06:09:43.053957031Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T06:09:43.053196117Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-2d366","title":"Foundation: Define SwarmConfig struct and TOML schema","description":"## Purpose

Define the core configuration structure for the weighted multi-project swarm system. This struct will be embedded in the main NTM config and loaded from `config.toml`.

## Implementation

Create `internal/config/swarm.go` with:

```go
// SwarmConfig configures the weighted multi-project agent swarm system.
type SwarmConfig struct {
    // DefaultScanDir is the base directory to scan for projects (e.g., "/dp")
    DefaultScanDir string `toml:"default_scan_dir"`
    
    // Tier thresholds for bead-based allocation
    Tier1Threshold int `toml:"tier1_threshold"` // Default: 400
    Tier2Threshold int `toml:"tier2_threshold"` // Default: 100
    
    // Allocation per tier: {cc, cod, gmi}
    Tier1Allocation AllocationSpec `toml:"tier1_allocation"`
    Tier2Allocation AllocationSpec `toml:"tier2_allocation"`
    Tier3Allocation AllocationSpec `toml:"tier3_allocation"`
    
    // Session structure
    SessionsPerType int `toml:"sessions_per_type"` // Default: 3
    
    // Timing
    StaggerDelayMs int `toml:"stagger_delay_ms"` // Default: 300
    
    // Account rotation
    AutoRotateAccounts bool `toml:"auto_rotate_accounts"`
    
    // Limit detection patterns per agent type
    LimitPatterns map[string][]string `toml:"limit_patterns"`
    
    // Marching orders templates
    MarchingOrders MarchingOrdersConfig `toml:"marching_orders"`
}

type AllocationSpec struct {
    CC  int `toml:"cc"`
    Cod int `toml:"cod"`
    Gmi int `toml:"gmi"`
}

type MarchingOrdersConfig struct {
    Default string `toml:"default"`
    Review  string `toml:"review"`
}

// Defaults returns SwarmConfig with sensible defaults
func (c *SwarmConfig) Defaults() SwarmConfig {
    return SwarmConfig{
        DefaultScanDir:  "/dp",
        Tier1Threshold:  400,
        Tier2Threshold:  100,
        Tier1Allocation: AllocationSpec{CC: 4, Cod: 4, Gmi: 2},
        Tier2Allocation: AllocationSpec{CC: 3, Cod: 3, Gmi: 2},
        Tier3Allocation: AllocationSpec{CC: 1, Cod: 1, Gmi: 1},
        SessionsPerType: 3,
        StaggerDelayMs:  300,
        LimitPatterns: map[string][]string{
            "cc":  {"You've hit your limit", "usage limit"},
            "cod": {"You've hit your usage limit"},
            "gmi": {"quota exceeded"},
        },
    }
}
```

## Integration

1. Add `Swarm SwarmConfig` field to main Config struct in `internal/config/config.go`
2. Ensure TOML unmarshaling works correctly
3. Add validation for thresholds (tier1 > tier2 > 0)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] SwarmConfig struct defined with all fields
- [ ] Defaults() method returns sensible defaults
- [ ] Integrated into main Config struct
- [ ] TOML schema documented in comments
- [ ] Unit tests for defaults and validation","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T06:57:53.819005935Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T07:36:07.378565888Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T07:36:07.378520292Z","close_reason":"Implemented SwarmConfig struct with tier-based allocation, defaults, validation, and TOML support. Created swarm.go and swarm_test.go. All tests pass. Integrated into main Config struct.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2d366","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:19:18.123342089Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2dovu","title":"EPIC: Robot Mode Ergonomics + Token Efficiency (TOON + JSON)","description":"## Goal
Make robot mode maximally ergonomic and token‑efficient for AI agents while preserving first‑class JSON support for tooling.

## Rationale
Robot mode is consumed primarily by AI agents (Codex/Claude/Gemini) where tokens and readability directly affect cost and accuracy. JSON is reliable for tooling but verbose; TOON can be far more compact for uniform arrays. We should support both and choose defaults based on evidence.

## Ground Truth
- `RobotResponse` already exists in `internal/robot/types.go` and is the canonical envelope.
- JSON output is currently pretty‑printed (stable and easy to diff) but not token‑efficient.

## Scope
- Evaluate TOON vs JSON for NTM robot payloads (token savings, clarity, failure modes).
- Add a robot output **format selector** (`json`, `toon`, `auto`) with config/env overrides.
- Standardize/validate the robot output envelope (reuse existing struct; no new schema).
- Provide token‑efficient profiles (terse vs full) **without** breaking parseability.
- Add pagination/limits where payloads are large.

## Non‑Goals
- No breaking removal of JSON.
- No dependency on a specific external CLI if not installed.

## Success Criteria
- Agents can request TOON output when beneficial.
- JSON remains first‑class and stable for scripts.
- Robot outputs are consistent and easier for LLMs to parse.
- Unit + E2E tests cover format selector, envelopes, and large payloads.","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-2dyqk","title":"caut: Implement background polling for usage data","description":"Create background goroutine that polls caut for usage data.

## Requirements

1. Poll caut at configured interval (default 60s)
2. Parse JSON output into internal structs
3. Update in-memory cache for dashboard
4. Trigger alerts when thresholds exceeded

## Implementation

```go
package caut

type UsagePoller struct {
    config   *config.CautConfig
    cache    *UsageCache
    interval time.Duration
    stopCh   chan struct{}
    alertCh  chan Alert
}

func (p *UsagePoller) Start() {
    go p.pollLoop()
}

func (p *UsagePoller) pollLoop() {
    ticker := time.NewTicker(p.interval)
    defer ticker.Stop()
    
    for {
        select {
        case <-ticker.C:
            usage, err := p.fetchUsage()
            if err != nil {
                // Log error, continue
                continue
            }
            p.cache.Update(usage)
            p.checkAlerts(usage)
        case <-p.stopCh:
            return
        }
    }
}

func (p *UsagePoller) fetchUsage() (*Usage, error) {
    cmd := exec.Command("caut", "status", "--json")
    // Parse output
}
```

## Code Location
- internal/integrations/caut/poller.go
- internal/integrations/caut/cache.go

## Testing
- Mock caut output, verify parsing
- Verify cache updates
- Verify alert triggering","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:59:05.756603440Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T16:50:02.306935382Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T16:50:02.306888674Z","close_reason":"Implemented background polling with UsagePoller and UsageCache in internal/integrations/caut/","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2dyqk","depends_on_id":"bd-2lsyy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:59:05.861524087Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2dyqk","depends_on_id":"bd-c749y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:00:14.889118196Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2dyqk","depends_on_id":"bd-d9jwi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:00:21.365802559Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2eblo","title":"Task: Implement conflict density metric","description":"# Task: Implement conflict density metric

Track disagreements per output pair.

## Implementation
```go
// internal/ensemble/conflicts.go
type ConflictTracker struct {
    Conflicts []Conflict
}

type Conflict struct {
    Topic      string
    ModeA      string
    ModeB      string
    PositionA  string
    PositionB  string
    Severity   ConflictSeverity  // minor, moderate, major
    Resolved   bool
    Resolution string
}

type ConflictDensity struct {
    TotalConflicts      int
    ResolvedConflicts   int
    UnresolvedConflicts int
    ConflictsPerPair    float64
    HighConflictPairs   []string
    Source              string // "auditor" or "fallback"
}

func NewConflictTracker() *ConflictTracker
func (c *ConflictTracker) FromAudit(report *AuditReport) []Conflict
func (c *ConflictTracker) DetectConflicts(outputs []ModeOutput) []Conflict // fallback
func (c *ConflictTracker) GetDensity(totalPairs int) *ConflictDensity
func (c *ConflictTracker) GetHighConflictPairs(threshold int) []string
```

## Conflict Detection
- **Primary source:** DisagreementAuditor report (preferred)
- **Fallback:** heuristic from ModeOutput when auditor not run
  - contradictory theses (exact/normalized mismatch)
  - conflicting risk impact for same risk text
  - opposing recommendations on same topic

## Logging Requirements
- Log source (auditor vs fallback)
- Log counts + high-conflict pairs

## Tests
- Unit: density math for N conflicts across K pairs
- Unit: auditor → conflict conversion
- Unit: fallback detection on synthetic outputs

 {"id":"bd-lfwpg","title":"Task: Integrate EnsembleManager with SessionOrchestrator in internal/swarm/","description":"# Task: Integrate EnsembleManager with SessionOrchestrator

## Location
internal/swarm/orchestrator.go (extend)
internal/swarm/ensemble_integration.go (new)

## Purpose
Hook EnsembleManager into the existing swarm infrastructure so ensemble spawning leverages existing session creation, agent launching, and prompt injection patterns.

## Integration Points

### 1. SessionOrchestrator Extension
```go
// Add to SessionOrchestrator
type SessionOrchestrator struct {
    // ... existing fields
    ensembleManager *ensemble.EnsembleManager
}

// New method for ensemble-aware session creation
func (o *SessionOrchestrator) CreateEnsembleSession(cfg ensemble.EnsembleConfig) (*OrchestrationResult, error) {
    // Delegate to EnsembleManager which handles:
    // - Session creation
    // - Pane splitting
    // - Agent launching
    // - Mode assignment
    // - Preamble injection
    return o.ensembleManager.SpawnEnsemble(cfg)
}
```

### 2. SwarmPlan Extension
```go
// Extend SwarmPlan to support ensemble
type SwarmPlan struct {
    // ... existing fields
    Ensemble *EnsemblePlan `json:'ensemble,omitempty'`
}

type EnsemblePlan struct {
    Question    string
    Preset      string
    Modes       []string
    AgentMix    map[string]int
    Synthesis   string
}
```

### 3. AgentLauncher Integration
Ensure EnsembleManager uses existing AgentLauncher patterns:
- Same launch delays
- Same post-launch verification
- Same error handling

### 4. PromptInjector Integration
Ensure EnsembleManager uses extended PromptInjector:
- Mode-aware injection
- Same stagger delays
- Same rate limit awareness

## Wiring in main/startup
```go
// In startup or cmd initialization
func initializeEnsemble(cfg *config.Config, tmuxClient *tmux.Client, stateStore *state.Store) *ensemble.EnsembleManager {
    catalog, _ := ensemble.NewModeCatalog()
    catalog.LoadAll()
    
    preambleEngine, _ := ensemble.NewPreambleEngine()
    // Register all mode preambles...
    
    return ensemble.NewEnsembleManager(catalog, preambleEngine, tmuxClient, stateStore)
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] EnsembleManager uses existing orchestrator patterns
- [ ] SwarmPlan can include ensemble configuration
- [ ] Agent launching follows existing delays/verification
- [ ] Prompt injection follows existing patterns
- [ ] No duplicate code - reuse existing infrastructure
- [ ] Clean separation of concerns","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:36:21.017163133Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T16:46:57.433260881Z","closed_at":"2026-01-24T16:46:57.433242316Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lfwpg","depends_on_id":"bd-2pb6e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:38:42.974698798Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-lfwpg","depends_on_id":"bd-lp5h4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:38:38.435827653Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-ljrn","title":"EPIC: Proactive Context Window Management with Predictive Compaction","description":"**IDEA #4 - ELIMINATES SURPRISES (88% confidence)**

## What It Does
Instead of reacting when an agent hits context exhaustion (80-95% full), NTM predicts when exhaustion will occur and proactively triggers compaction before the agent's work is interrupted.

## Why This Matters
- Currently: Agents suddenly hit context limits, lose mid-work context
- After: NTM handles it proactively; work continues smoothly
- Users never see 'Conversation compacted - lost context' mid-task
- Transforms context limits from a constant worry to a non-issue

## How It Works
Continuous monitoring:
1. Track token velocity: Characters per minute → estimated tokens per minute
2. Model context growth: Based on recent velocity, project time-to-exhaustion
3. Predict exhaustion: 'At current rate, agent will exhaust context in 12 minutes'
4. Proactive action: At 70% + high velocity, suggest compaction; at 75% with continued high velocity, trigger automatically

## Prediction Model
tokens_remaining = context_limit - current_tokens
time_to_exhaustion = tokens_remaining / token_velocity_per_minute

if time_to_exhaustion < threshold_minutes:
    trigger_proactive_compaction()

## Flywheel Alignment
- Enhances existing context rotation feature
- Pairs perfectly with Smart Session Recovery
- Compaction + recovery = seamless context management

## Technical Implementation
- New file: internal/context/predictor.go
- ContextPredictor struct with velocity sampling
- Dashboard integration for context status display
- Config under [context_rotation.prediction] section

## Dependencies
Depends on: Smart Session Recovery (for post-compaction context restoration)","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-lo0ko","title":"Task: Add memory_mb field for process memory usage","description":"## What

Add `memory_mb` field showing the resident memory usage of the agent process.

## Why

Runaway processes can consume excessive memory, eventually causing OOM kills
or system slowdown. Early detection allows proactive intervention.

## Implementation

```go
func getProcessMemoryMB(pid int) (int, error) {
    data, err := os.ReadFile(fmt.Sprintf("/proc/%d/status", pid))
    if err != nil {
        return 0, err
    }
    
    for _, line := range strings.Split(string(data), "\n") {
        if strings.HasPrefix(line, "VmRSS:") {
            // Format: "VmRSS:    123456 kB"
            parts := strings.Fields(line)
            if len(parts) >= 2 {
                kb, _ := strconv.Atoi(parts[1])
                return kb / 1024, nil  // Convert to MB
            }
        }
    }
    return 0, nil
}
```

## Output Schema

```json
{
  "panes": [{
    "index": 2,
    "child_pid": 12346,
    "memory_mb": 450,
    "memory_warning": false  // true if > threshold
  }]
}
```

## Thresholds

- Normal: < 500 MB
- Warning: 500-1000 MB
- Critical: > 1000 MB

These thresholds should be configurable.

## Alternative: Total Process Tree Memory

For more accuracy, sum memory of child_pid and all its descendants:

```go
func getProcessTreeMemoryMB(pid int) int {
    total := getProcessMemoryMB(pid)
    children := getChildPIDs(pid)
    for _, child := range children {
        total += getProcessTreeMemoryMB(child)
    }
    return total
}
```

## Testing

- Unit test: Mock /proc reads
- Integration test: Verify against known process memory","notes":"Feature already implemented in status_enrich.go. getProcessMemoryMB function reads VmRSS from /proc/<pid>/status and enrichAgentStatus populates MemoryMB field.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:19:26.830905564Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T02:40:09.515349481Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T02:40:09.515258209Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lo0ko","depends_on_id":"bd-1g9wv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:19:26.835672001Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-lp5h4","title":"Task: Implement EnsembleManager core logic in internal/ensemble/manager.go","description":"# Task: Implement EnsembleManager Core Logic

## Location
internal/ensemble/manager.go

## Purpose
Central coordinator for reasoning ensemble sessions. Orchestrates the full lifecycle: spawn -> assign -> inject -> monitor -> synthesize.

## Key Types
```go
type EnsembleConfig struct {
    SessionName string
    Question    string
    Ensemble    string   // built-in or user-defined ensemble name
    Modes       []string // explicit mode IDs (if not using ensemble)

    AgentMix    map[string]int
    Assignment  string // round-robin, affinity, explicit

    Synthesis   SynthesisConfig // strategy + synthesizer_mode
    Budget      BudgetConfig    // per_agent/total/synthesis/context
    Cache       CacheConfig     // context pack cache control
    EarlyStop   EarlyStopConfig // marginal utility stop
}
```

## Implementation Outline
- Resolve modes from ensemble or explicit list
- Validate config (strategy, budget caps, mode IDs/codes)
- Create tmux session + panes
- Assign modes to panes
- Launch agents and inject preambles
- Persist ensemble state

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] SpawnEnsemble creates session with correct agent/mode mapping
- [ ] Mode resolution works for ensembles and explicit lists
- [ ] Config validation errors are actionable
- [ ] State persisted to enable status queries
- [ ] Errors handled gracefully (partial success possible)
- [ ] Logging comprehensive for debugging","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:36:11.733848210Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T16:08:45.712037150Z","closed_at":"2026-01-24T16:08:45.712017212Z","close_reason":"Implemented EnsembleManager core logic (config validation, mode resolution, tmux spawn, assignment, preamble injection, state persistence).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lp5h4","depends_on_id":"bd-1ovi8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T10:02:15.343715793Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-lp5h4","depends_on_id":"bd-2pb6e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T09:58:51.344687950Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-lp5h4","depends_on_id":"bd-2qfa5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:52:53.751919785Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-lp5h4","depends_on_id":"bd-35e81","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T09:40:41.984611596Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-lp5h4","depends_on_id":"bd-4io74","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:38:28.680458975Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-lp5h4","depends_on_id":"bd-qn9gc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:38:24.070856568Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
